formula:0008.note: Reconstructed TABL step 2; the T-by-T temporal matrix produces an energy matrix with the same D-prime-by-T shape.

formula:0015.latex: \frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf W_1}=\frac{\partial(\mathbf I_{D'}\mathbf W_1\mathbf X)}{\partial\mathbf W_1}=\mathbf X^{\mathrm T}\otimes\mathbf I_{D'}
formula:0015.note: Reconstructed by vectorizing W1 X; the Jacobian maps vec(W1) to vec(X-bar).

formula:0016.latex: \frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf X}=\frac{\partial(\mathbf W_1\mathbf X\mathbf I_T)}{\partial\mathbf X}=\mathbf I_T\otimes\mathbf W_1
formula:0016.paper_as_printed_latex: \frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf X}=\frac{\partial(\mathbf W_1\mathbf X\mathbf I_T)}{\partial\mathbf W_1}=\mathbf I_T\otimes\mathbf W_1
evidence: The left side is explicitly the Jacobian with respect to X.
Applying d vec(W1 X)/d vec(X) gives I_T tensor W1.
The printed middle denominator W1 conflicts with both the left side and the resulting Jacobian.
formula:0016.note: Corrected the differentiation variable in equation (17) from W1 to X.

formula:0017.latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}=\frac{\partial(\mathbf I_{D'}\bar{\mathbf X}\mathbf W)}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}=\mathbf W^{\mathrm T}\otimes\mathbf I_{D'}
formula:0017.note: Reconstructed by vectorizing E=X-bar W and differentiating with respect to X-bar.

formula:0018.latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\mathbf W}=\frac{\partial(\bar{\mathbf X}\mathbf W\mathbf I_T)}{\partial\mathbf W}=\mathbf I_T\otimes\bar{\mathbf X}
formula:0018.paper_as_printed_latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\mathbf W}=\frac{\partial(\bar{\mathbf X}\mathbf W\mathbf I_T)}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}=\mathbf I_T\otimes\bar{\mathbf X}
evidence: The left side requests the Jacobian with respect to W.
Applying d vec(X-bar W)/d vec(W) gives I_T tensor X-bar.
The printed middle denominator X-bar conflicts with the left side and final result.
formula:0018.note: Corrected the differentiation variable in equation (19) from X-bar to W.

formula:0019.note: Reconstructed the diagonal derivative of the row-wise softmax.

evidence: Equation (9) defines alpha as a standard softmax across the second index.
Direct quotient-rule differentiation gives -alpha_ij alpha_ip for distinct coordinates in the same row.
The printed expression has the wrong sign and can have an empty denominator when T=2.
formula:0020.note: Replaced the printed off-diagonal softmax derivative with the standard negative cross-coordinate derivative.

formula:0021.note: Reconstructed the zero derivative between different independently normalized feature rows.

formula:0022.latex: \begin{aligned}\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf A}&=\lambda\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\bar{\mathbf X})),\\\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}&=\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\lambda\mathbf A+(1-\lambda)\mathbf 1_{D'\times T}))+\lambda\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\bar{\mathbf X}))\frac{\partial\mathbf A}{\partial\mathbf E}\frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}.\end{aligned}
formula:0022.paper_as_printed_latex: \begin{aligned}\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf A}&=\lambda\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\bar{\mathbf X})),\\\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}&=\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\lambda\mathbf A+(1-\lambda)\mathbf 1_{D'T}))+\operatorname{diag}(\operatorname{vec}(\bar{\mathbf X}))\odot\left(\frac{\partial\mathbf A}{\partial\mathbf E}\frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}\right).\end{aligned}
evidence: Vectorizing an element-wise product gives a diagonal matrix multiplying the other factor's Jacobian by ordinary matrix multiplication.
Because A is multiplied by lambda in X-tilde, its dependency on X-bar also carries a factor lambda.
Direct differential expansion of X-tilde confirms both the ordinary Jacobian product and the missing lambda.
formula:0022.note: Corrected equation (24) to use Jacobian matrix multiplication and restored the missing factor lambda in the attention-dependent term.

formula:0023.note: Reconstructed by differentiating the attention interpolation with A and X-bar held fixed with respect to lambda.

formula:0024.latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}=\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\left(\mathbf W_2^{\mathrm T}\otimes\mathbf I_{D'}\right),\qquad\bar{\mathbf Y}=\widetilde{\mathbf X}\mathbf W_2+\mathbf B
formula:0024.note: Reconstructed the activation Jacobian composed with the vectorized temporal projection.

formula:0025.latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf B}=\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\left(\mathbf I_{T'}\otimes\mathbf I_{D'}\right)
formula:0025.paper_as_printed_latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf B}=\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\left(\mathbf I_T\otimes\mathbf I_{D'}\right)
evidence: TABL defines Y and B with shape D-prime by T-prime.
The bias Jacobian is therefore the identity on D-prime times T-prime output coordinates.
Using I_T is dimensionally invalid whenever the output horizon T-prime differs from the input length T.
formula:0025.note: Corrected the output-time identity from I_T to I_T-prime in the bias Jacobian.

formula:0026.latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf W_2}=\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\left(\mathbf I_{T'}\otimes\widetilde{\mathbf X}\right)
formula:0026.paper_as_printed_latex: \frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf W_2}=\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\left(\mathbf I_T\otimes\widetilde{\mathbf X}\right)
evidence: W2 maps T input positions to T-prime output positions and has shape T by T-prime.
Vectorizing X-tilde W2 yields the Jacobian I_{T-prime} tensor X-tilde.
The printed I_T only works accidentally when T equals T-prime.
formula:0026.note: Corrected the output-time identity from I_T to I_T-prime in the W2 Jacobian.

formula:0027.latex: \frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf W_1}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf Y}\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}\frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf W_1}
formula:0027.note: Reconstructed the full chain from loss through output, attention interpolation, and the first bilinear projection.

formula:0028.latex: \frac{\partial L}{\partial\lambda}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf Y}\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\lambda}
formula:0028.note: Reconstructed the chain-rule gradient for the attention interpolation scalar lambda.

formula:0029.latex: \frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf W}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf Y}\frac{\partial\phi(\bar{\mathbf Y})}{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}\frac{\partial\bar{\mathbf Y}}{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}\frac{\partial\widetilde{\mathbf X}}{\partial\mathbf A}\frac{\partial\mathbf A}{\partial\mathbf E}\frac{\partial\mathbf E}{\partial\mathbf W}
formula:0029.note: Reconstructed the chain-rule gradient through the temporal attention matrix W.

formula:0030.latex: \frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf W_2}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf Y}\frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf W_2}
formula:0030.note: Reconstructed the direct chain-rule gradient for the final temporal projection.

formula:0031.latex: \frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf B}=\frac{\partial L}{\partial\mathbf Y}\frac{\partial\mathbf Y}{\partial\mathbf B}
formula:0031.note: Reconstructed the direct chain-rule gradient for the output bias.

formula:0013.latex: \mathbf f_t=\sigma(\mathbf W_{xf}\mathbf x_t+\mathbf W_{hf}\mathbf h_{t-1}+\mathbf b_f)
formula:0013.paper_as_printed_latex: \mathbf f_t=\sigma(\mathbf W_{xf}\mathbf x+\mathbf W_{hf}\mathbf h_{t-1}+\mathbf b_f)
evidence: The equation defines the forget gate at time t and the surrounding prose identifies the current time-step input.
The candidate-cell equation in the same recurrence explicitly uses x_t.
Using an unindexed x is inconsistent with the time-indexed LSTM recurrence.
formula:0013.note: Restored the missing time index on the current input to the forget gate.

formula:0014.latex: \mathbf i_t=\sigma(\mathbf W_{xi}\mathbf x_t+\mathbf W_{hi}\mathbf h_{t-1}+\mathbf b_i)

evidence: The equation defines the input gate at time t and depends on the current time-step input.
The neighboring candidate-cell equation explicitly uses x_t.
Using an unindexed x is inconsistent with the recurrent indexing used throughout the block.
formula:0014.note: Restored the missing time index on the current input to the input gate.

formula:0015.latex: \mathbf c'_t=\tanh(\mathbf W_{hc}\mathbf h_{t-1}+\mathbf W_{xc}\mathbf x_t+\mathbf b_c)

formula:0017.latex: \mathbf o_t=\sigma(\mathbf W_{oc}\mathbf c_t+\mathbf W_{oh}\mathbf h_{t-1}+\mathbf b_o)
formula:0017.note: Reconstructed the paper's peephole-style output gate from the current cell and previous hidden state.

evidence: The same equation block uses sigma for logistic gates and tanh for cell-state content.
The standard LSTM exposed state gates tanh(c_t), while sigmoid(c_t) would constrain even fully open negative cell states to positive values.
The vector output gate and cell activation require an element-wise product.
formula:0018.note: Corrected the cell exposure from sigmoid to tanh and made the vector gate product explicit; this is a high-confidence LSTM-formulation correction, not an author-issued erratum.
